briefs.title: Council to consider new rules for determining tenant eligibility dates under Burnaby's TAP
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a set of criteria that staff would use to decide whether tenants keep their original eligibility date when a developer resubmits a rezoning application — a decision that affects which tenants qualify for moving help and compensation under the Tenant Assistance Policy (TAP).
briefs.action_required: If you are a landlord or tenant on a Burnaby development site, review the TAP criteria and watch for the council vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Would Change How Advisory Body Rules Are Set and Updated
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would change who controls the rules (called 'Terms of Reference') for City advisory bodies — giving the Mayor, Council, or the City's top administrator the power to request updates, with Council having final approval.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final adoption vote — this affects how Burnaby's advisory bodies operate.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024 Amendment No.1 — final adoption vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to temporarily cut plumbing permit fees for some developments
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would temporarily reduce plumbing permit fees for most developments (excluding small-scale multi-unit housing and tenant improvements) to $70 for the first fixture and $39 for each additional one, if the permit is issued by June 30, 2026. Owners who already paid the higher fees between January 1 and March 10, 2026 could receive a refund for the difference.
briefs.action_required: Apply for your plumbing permit before June 30, 2026 to lock in the lower temporary fee rate.

key_dates.label: Burnaby reduced plumbing permit fee deadline — permits must be issued by this date

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Would Set $500 Fines for Bear Spray Sales Violations
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would add $500 fines for retailers who sell bear spray to minors, skip ID checks, store it improperly, or fail to keep required sales records.
briefs.action_required: Review your bear spray storage and record-keeping practices now — fines of $500 per violation may be coming.

key_dates.label: Burnaby bear spray sales enforcement bylaw — final adoption vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Bylaw Would Restrict Who Can Sell Bear Spray
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would limit bear spray sales in Burnaby to outdoor and sporting goods retailers only, with strict ID checks, age limits, and record-keeping rules.
briefs.action_required: If you sell bear spray, check whether your business qualifies as an 'authorized vendor' under the proposed rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Bear Spray Regulations bylaw — final adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of mid-rise rental building at 5889 Barker Avenue, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on the final adoption of a rezoning bylaw for a mid-rise rental apartment building at 5889 Barker Avenue in the Metrotown area. The proposed bylaw would allow Concord Greenhouse (Nominee) Ltd. to move forward with a development that includes 106 rental units, 48 of which would be non-market (below-market-rate) rentals.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on March 10, 2026 — it could affect nearby property values and rental supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ 25-15 rezoning bylaw final adoption vote — Council meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on rezoning at Barker + Olive development site
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would change the zoning — the rules about how land can be used and what can be built — for a site in Burnaby known as 'Barker + Olive,' updating the development plan on file.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council adoption decision in 2026 if your business is near the Barker + Olive development site.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Amendment Bylaw No. 14782 — council adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of six-storey self-storage facility at Still Creek Avenue
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on final adoption of a rezoning bylaw that would allow a six-storey self-storage building with underground parking at 3942–3944 Still Creek Avenue. All required conditions — including environmental, flood, and servicing plans — have been met by the applicant.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 10, 2026 council decision if this development affects your business or property nearby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ 25-05 rezoning bylaw final adoption vote — council meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to consider: Rezoning approval for self-storage expansion at 3942–3944 Still Creek, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would amend Burnaby's zoning rules to allow an expanded self-storage facility at 3942 and 3944 Still Creek Avenue, based on updated development plans prepared by Studio One Architecture Inc.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council adoption vote — it could affect nearby property use and development plans.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Amendment Bylaw No. 14763 — final adoption vote scheduled

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of rental tower and community hub in Edmonds
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on final adoption of a rezoning bylaw that would allow a residential rental tower with a community hub to be built at 7135 Walker Ave. and 7244 Arcola St. in the Edmonds neighbourhood. The developer, Gracorp Properties LP, has met all required conditions set by the City.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the March 10, 2026 council decision — it could affect nearby property and business owners in Edmonds.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ 24-20 rezoning bylaw — final adoption council vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rezoning of Two Properties at Walker and Arcola Streets
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone two properties — 7135 Walker and 7244 Arcola — changing what can legally be built or operated there. If adopted, any development on these sites must follow new guidelines attached to the bylaw.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the final council adoption vote — this rezoning could affect nearby property use and development plans.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw No. 14744 — third reading passed, awaiting final adoption

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendment Would Create New Employment, Industrial, and Agricultural Districts
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a major update to Burnaby's Zoning Bylaw that would create new land-use zones — including Employment (E1), Industrial (I1), Interim Parks (PM1), Interim Institutional (PM2), and Interim Agricultural (AM1) districts — and update dozens of definitions covering businesses like catering kitchens, auto body shops, animal care, and short-term rentals.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business property could be affected by a new zoning district and attend any upcoming council meetings to have your say.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 2, 2026 — third reading scheduled

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to switch public EV chargers from time-based to energy-based fees
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to charge EV drivers by the amount of electricity used (per kWh) instead of by the hour at City-owned public charging stations. The proposal also includes idle fees to free up spots after a vehicle is fully charged.
briefs.action_required: If you use Burnaby's public EV chargers, watch for the council decision and the 30-day notice before new rates take effect.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — proposed EV energy-based user fees

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to add $75 fine for overstaying time limit at EV charging parking spots
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would add a new fine for drivers who exceed the maximum time limit at electric vehicle (EV) metered parking spaces in Burnaby. The proposed penalty is $75, reduced to $60 if paid early.
briefs.action_required: If your business has EV charging spaces, watch for this rule — it affects how long customers can park there.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging fees at city parking meters
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw that would replace the current EV charging fee table with two new rate structures — one based on energy used (per kWh) and one based on time parked — plus idle fees for vehicles that stay plugged in after charging.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business uses or offers Burnaby city EV charging stations — new rates may affect your costs.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update EV charging meter rules, adding energy-based fees and time limits
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a bylaw amendment that would update how public electric vehicle (EV) charging meters work in Burnaby — allowing fees based on energy used (not just time), and setting maximum parking time limits at EV charging spots.
briefs.action_required: If you operate or use public EV charging spaces in Burnaby, watch for the council vote and review the new fee and time-limit rules.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby RCMP Crime Stats Show Mixed Results for Q3 2025
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review the Burnaby RCMP's quarterly performance report for July–September 2025. The report shows good news for some crimes — like auto theft and fraud — but flags sharp rises in theft from vehicles (up 45%), residential break-ins (up 59%), and drug trafficking (up 123%).
briefs.action_required: Review the theft-from-vehicle and break-and-enter trends — consider upgrading your business security now.

briefs.title: Council to consider Burnaby RCMP Public Safety Report: Aug–Nov 2025 activities
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report from Burnaby RCMP covering policing activity from August to November 2025, including enforcement actions against gang activity, robberies, impaired driving, and community outreach programs.
briefs.action_required: Review the report if your business operates near Metrotown, Kingsway, or Lougheed — these areas saw elevated enforcement.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby RCMP Policing Update for August–November 2025
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive an information report from the RCMP covering policing activity from August to November 2025 and a performance plan update from July to September 2025. No new rules or bylaws are proposed — this is a routine update for Council's awareness.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Burnaby Fire Department Status Report: Rising Calls, Inspections & Training
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Public Safety Committee is set to review the Fire Department's status report covering activity through December 2025. The report shows total incidents rose to 17,899 in 2025, with medical emergencies making up 57% of calls, and fire inspections of businesses and buildings continuing across the city.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in Burnaby, ensure your fire safety plan and alarm systems are current — inspections are ongoing.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Food Service, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Fire Department Status Update for December 2025
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a routine status update from the Burnaby Fire Department covering activity through the end of December 2025. No new rules or requirements are being proposed — this is an information-only report.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Survey Results from Burnaby's Online Child Safety Workshop
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will review survey results from a November 2025 workshop on combatting online sexual exploitation of youth. The report summarizes feedback from attendees and suggests topics for future workshops — no new rules or bylaws are being proposed at this time.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Status Update on Youth Sexual Exploitation Task Force
briefs.summary: Council will consider receiving a status update on the Burnaby–New Westminster Task Force on Sexually Exploited and At-Risk Youth, which coordinates local agencies, schools, and police to protect vulnerable young people from sexual exploitation.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Updates on Major Burnaby Civic Construction Projects and Staffing Plans
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive status updates on seven major civic building projects — including new recreation centres, a theatre, and an RCMP building — plus a plan to hire and train roughly 900 staff needed to run the new facilities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for council decisions on facility openings and budgets — new recreation centres could affect local hiring and programming opportunities.

key_dates.label: Mintara Restaurant Expansion — project handover to Parks, Recreation and Culture
Community Facilities Fit-Out Project — initial procurement processes planned
James Cowan Theatre — project handover to Parks, Recreation and Culture
Community Safety Building (RCMP) — construction completion anticipated
Burnaby Lake Recreation Centre — project handover to Parks, Recreation and Culture
Cameron Community Centre and Library — project handover to Parks, Recreation and Culture
Brentwood Community Centre — construction start anticipated
Brentwood Community Centre — project handover to Parks, Recreation and Culture
People-Play staffing plan — phased launch scenarios presented to Council

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Labour & Employment, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to consider Burnaby's 2025 Gaming Reserve spending plan
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving Burnaby's 2025 Gaming Reserve budget, which outlines how roughly $13.6M in casino grants and interest will be spent on community programs, city events, environmental projects, and capital upgrades — with a projected year-end balance of about $11.7M.
briefs.action_required: Review the spending priorities to see if any funded programs affect your business or neighbourhood.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Gaming Reserve Financial Summary 2000–2025
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a financial summary of its Gaming Reserve — a special fund built from casino revenue — covering 2000 to 2025. The report shows the reserve has received nearly $239M in casino grants and spent over $258M on city capital and operating projects, leaving a balance of about $11.7M.
briefs.action_required: Review the report if your business depends on city-funded infrastructure or community grants supported by gaming revenue.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Status Update on Burnaby's 2025 Casino Gaming Reserve Fund
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to receive a status update on its Gaming Reserve — a fund built from casino revenue — showing $12.7M spent in 2025 and $11.7M remaining. No new spending decisions are being made yet; this report is for information only.

briefs.title: Council to consider: 2025 Burnaby Mountain Safety Fund spending report
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report showing how $6.68 million from the Burnaby Mountain Safety Reserve Fund was spent in 2025 on three safety projects, leaving a balance of about $3.32 million.
briefs.action_required: Review the report if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain — these safety upgrades may affect local access and emergency response.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to consider: Status update on Burnaby Mountain Safety Reserve funded by Trans Mountain
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a status update on how Burnaby is spending $20.1 million from Trans Mountain Corporation over 21 years for safety projects on Burnaby Mountain, including a new fire station, hazardous materials vehicle, and wildfire detection.
briefs.action_required: Monitor how these safety investments near Burnaby Mountain may affect your business operations or emergency planning.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Mountain Safety Reserve annual report deadline — Trans Mountain agreement
Trans Mountain installment #3 payment to Burnaby — $5M due

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Natural Resources

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Supporting Tourism Burnaby's Bid to Host 2028 Urban Forest Conference
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on supporting Tourism Burnaby's plan to submit a bid to host the 2028 Canadian Urban Forest Conference. No city funding is being requested — just an official 'in principle' endorsement so Tourism Burnaby can meet the May bid deadline.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — hospitality and event businesses may see opportunity if Burnaby wins the bid.

key_dates.label: Tourism Burnaby — 2028 CUFC bid submission deadline

briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service, Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Consider 25-Year Parks, Recreation and Culture Master Plan
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on 'Making Life Better,' a draft 25-year plan that would guide all City spending and decisions on parks, recreation facilities, and cultural programs in Burnaby through 2050, covering 15 service areas and 25 specific actions.
briefs.action_required: Review the draft plan and submit feedback before Council votes — it shapes parks, facilities, and cultural funding near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Making Life Better PRC Plan — Council consideration date

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's new long-range Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a draft plan called 'Making Life Better' that would set the direction for parks, recreation, and culture services over the next 10–15 years, covering 15 service areas and 25 priority actions. If approved, a second round of public engagement would follow in spring 2026 before the final plan is adopted.
briefs.action_required: Participate in the upcoming public engagement sessions this spring to share your input before the final plan is approved.

key_dates.label: Burnaby PRC Plan — Council vote at regular meeting
Burnaby PRC Plan — second round public engagement begins
Burnaby PRC Plan — PRCC engagement results review meeting

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider updated Community Grant application form for 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to review a revised Community Grant application form that non-profit and community organizations would use to apply for city funding in 2026. The updated form covers operating grants, initiative grants, recreational sport grants, and food security grants.
briefs.action_required: If your non-profit relies on City of Burnaby grants, review the revised application form and prepare your documents early.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider updated Community Grant Policy for non-profits
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a revised Community Grant Policy that would set the rules for how non-profit organizations apply for City of Burnaby funding — including operating, initiative, and recreational sports grants — with application windows in April and October each year.
briefs.action_required: If your non-profit relies on City grants, review the draft policy now to confirm you still meet the eligibility rules.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Community Grant Policy — April application window opens
Burnaby Community Grant Policy — October application window opens

briefs.title: Council to Consider Updates to Burnaby Community Grant Policy and Application Form
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on changes to its Community Grant Policy and application form that would clarify what grants can and cannot be used for, tighten eligibility rules for Food Security grants, and update how funds are shared when demand exceeds the available budget.
briefs.action_required: If you run a Burnaby non-profit, review the proposed policy changes before they take effect.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Community Grant Policy amendments — Council vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $580,985 ServiceNow IT ticketing software licence renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on renewing the City's IT ticketing software licences directly with ServiceNow, Inc. for roughly $581,000 (taxes included), covering March 2026 to March 2029.

key_dates.label: Burnaby ServiceNow IT licence contract start date
Burnaby ServiceNow IT licence contract end date

briefs.title: Council to Consider Applying for Up to $200,000 in Provincial Development Approvals Grant
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to apply for up to $200,000 in provincial grant funding to speed up the city's development approvals process, including faster permits for small housing projects and modular construction.
briefs.action_required: If you build or develop in Burnaby, watch for faster permit pathways that may result from this grant.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — UBCM LGDAP 2026 grant application authorization

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: Statutory Right of Way at 4111 Ledger Avenue, Burnaby — Council to Consider
briefs.summary: Council is set to consider approving a proposed statutory right of way — a legal strip of land that gives the city specific access or use rights — over a portion of the property at 4111 Ledger Avenue in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate near 4111 Ledger Avenue, review the sketches and monitor the council decision.

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting BC Hydro access to City land at 4111 Norland Avenue
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on allowing BC Hydro to access part of a City-owned property at 4111 Norland Avenue. This access — called a Statutory Right-of-Way, meaning a legal permission to use someone else's land — would let BC Hydro install and maintain electrical infrastructure needed to power the new Community Safety Building.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby urges Metro Vancouver to slow down Metro 2050 boundary expansion changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby's Mayor and Council have written to Metro Vancouver urging the regional board to pause four proposed changes to Metro 2050 — the regional growth plan — that would make it easier to expand the Urban Containment Boundary (the line that separates urban development from rural and agricultural land). Burnaby warns the changes could raise costs for builders, homebuyers, and transit users across the region.
briefs.action_required: Submit comments to Metro Vancouver before March 20, 2026 if your business could be affected by regional boundary or growth changes.

key_dates.label: Metro 2050 amendment options comment period closes — Metro Vancouver

briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver to Consider Loosening Urban Growth Boundary Rules
briefs.summary: Metro Vancouver is consulting on four proposed changes to Metro 2050 — the regional plan that controls where cities can grow. The changes would make it easier for Surrey, Langley Township, and Delta to expand housing, jobs, and services beyond current urban boundaries.
briefs.action_required: Submit comments to Metro Vancouver by March 20, 2026 if this affects your business or property.

key_dates.label: Metro 2050 amendment consultation comment period closes

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Agriculture

briefs.title: Council to vote on sending concerns about proposed Metro Vancouver regional growth plan changes
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on sending a letter to Metro Vancouver opposing four proposed changes to Metro 2050 — the regional plan that controls where urban growth can happen. The proposed changes would make it easier to expand the Urban Containment Boundary (the line that separates urban development from rural and agricultural land), which Burnaby says could raise costs for developers and transit users across the region.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Metro Vancouver Board decision in April 2026 — it could affect development costs and transit in your area.

key_dates.label: Metro 2050 amendment comment period closes — Metro Vancouver Board
Metro Vancouver Board considers initiating Metro 2050 amendment process
Metro Vancouver Board considers final adoption of Metro 2050 amendment bylaw

briefs.title: Council to vote on liquor licence for Solo Karaoke on Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to recommend a liquor primary licence — meaning a licence where alcohol is the main service, not food — for Solo Karaoke at 6462 Kingsway. The business wants to serve alcohol daily from 9 a.m. to 2 a.m. for up to 80 people, and also allow minors in until 10 p.m. with a responsible adult.
briefs.action_required: Attend the November 25, 2025 Council meeting to speak for or against this licence application.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Solo Karaoke liquor primary licence vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider Liquor Licence Application for 102-6462 Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a liquor licence application (LLA 25-12) for a business at Unit 102-6462 Kingsway. A planning sketch dated August 21, 2025 has been prepared, but no decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect liquor-licensed businesses near 6462 Kingsway.

briefs.title: Council to vote on liquor licence for Solo Karaoke on Kingsway, Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to recommend a liquor primary licence — a permit allowing a business to serve alcohol as its main activity — for Solo Karaoke at 6462 Kingsway. City staff, RCMP, and provincial inspectors investigated earlier concerns and found no evidence of illegal liquor service, and now recommend Council support the application.
briefs.action_required: Watch the March 10, 2026 Council meeting outcome if this licence affects your business or neighbourhood.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Solo Karaoke liquor primary licence recommendation

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Solo Karaoke Ltd. Delegation Request in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to hear a delegation request from Solo Karaoke Ltd., meaning the business has asked for time to speak directly to Council. The outcome of this presentation could affect how the business operates in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it may affect solo karaoke business operations in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Solo Karaoke Ltd. delegation — Burnaby Council meeting date

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to regulate bear spray sales and raise parking fees
briefs.summary: At its February 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby Council voted on several proposals affecting local businesses, including new rules for selling bear spray, higher on-street parking fees, and a renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area for 2026–2031.
briefs.action_required: Check if your business sells bear spray — new licence and enforcement rules are moving forward.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal period begins
Burnaby outdoor special events season opens — noise bylaw exemptions in effect
Burnaby outdoor special events season closes — noise bylaw exemptions end

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to Replace Entire Zoning Bylaw, Effective July 1, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a full rewrite of its Zoning Bylaw — the rulebook that controls what can be built and where. The proposed bylaw would introduce new rules on building size, parking, landscaping, and tenant protections, with a target effective date of July 1, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April/May Council meeting — the new Zoning Bylaw will be introduced and could affect your property or business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby new Zoning Bylaw introduced at Council — public review opportunity
Burnaby new Zoning Bylaw adoption at Council
Burnaby new Zoning Bylaw takes effect

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant
